common.name: Nome
common.languageHint: Protocolli, coaching Fan Assist e l'intera interfaccia usano questa lingua.
vol.title: StadiaChat · Volontario
vol.pendingBanner: Registrazione inviata. In attesa che l'Operations Lead dello stadio approvi l'accesso.
vol.seriousTimer: Timer incidente GRAVE: {s}s rimanenti fino all'override di sicurezza.
vol.empty: Chat operativa pronta. Chiedi FAQ di protocollo, segnala problemi sul campo o scala rischi critici.
vol.coachingFooter: Solo per te — non letto al tifoso
vol.photoHint: Foto opzionale se serve (pericolo, versamento, coda, danno) — max 25 MB, nessun ridimensionamento
ops.title: TERMINALE MASTER · StadiaChat
ops.planningHint: Pianifica i post per luogo — assegna volontari a gate, settori e strutture prima e durante il giorno di gara.
ops.planDescPh: es. Guidare ospiti ADA, far scorrere la coda, radio canale 1
ops.noPlans: Nessun piano ancora. Crea un post con un luogo di assistenza sopra.
ops.criticalFeedHint: Solo traffico grave / di emergenza (Cat D). La cronologia ops completa è su Timeline completa.
ops.noCritical: Nessun messaggio Categoria D al momento.
ops.timelineIncidentHint: Incidenti Cat C e Cat D aperti per questo stadio. Desk critico anche sulla pagina Cat D.
profile.title: Profilo
profile.subtitle: La preferenza di lingua si applica all'intera interfaccia e alle risposte dell'IA.
fan.title: Assistenza vocale tifoso
fan.subtitle: Punta il microfono al tifoso. L'IA rileva la lingua, risponde dai protocolli dello stadio e legge ad alta voce la risposta.
fan.coachingTitle: Assistenza volontario richiesta — le tue azioni (non parlate)
fan.typeFallback: O digita la domanda del tifoso se il microfono non è disponibile…
fan.unsupported: Questo browser non supporta il riconoscimento vocale. Usa Chrome/Edge su HTTPS o localhost, oppure digita la domanda del tifoso qui sotto.
fan.noSpeech: Nessun parlato catturato. Tieni il microfono e riprova.
fan.micDenied: Permesso microfono negato. Consenti l'accesso al microfono nel browser.
fan.notSecureTitle: Microfono bloccato su questo URL (Non sicuro)
fan.notSecure: I browser consentono il microfono solo su https:// o http://localhost — non su IP LAN come 192.168.x.x. Apri http://localhost:3000 per il microfono, o digita la domanda del tifoso qui sotto (il testo funziona sempre).
home.tagline: FIFA World Cup 2026 · AI Ops stadio
home.badge: Canale operativo sicuro · Non un chatbot pubblico per tifosi
home.hero1: AI per lo staff dello stadio che
home.hero2: le operazioni del giorno di gara
home.heroBody: StadiaChat solves FIFA World Cup 2026 stadium operations: multilingual volunteers, radio congestion, and mixed SOP/incident/emergency traffic. The AI Core answers protocols, escalates to the right Ops Lead, deploys emergency SOPs, enforces stadium tenancy, and runs a 300s safety override — with Fan Voice so volunteers help fans in any language.
home.sessionBound: Sessione legata a stadium_id · PIN · ruolo
home.regNoPin: Nessun PIN stadio necessario per registrarsi. Dopo l'approvazione dell'Ops Lead, accedi con il PIN dello stadio.
home.demoRegHint: In attesa finché l'Ops Lead approva — poi login con PIN.
home.demoOpsHint: metlife_2026 · ops_metlife_2026 · lingua salvata sul Lead
home.aboutTitle: Costruito per il caos del giorno di gara — non per demo di chatbot generiche
home.purposeBody: Dare a ogni volontario dello stadio un'interfaccia operativa veloce e contestuale: protocolli ufficiali nella loro lingua, escalation chiara all'Operations Lead e percorsi di sicurezza critici che non aspettano mai un canale radio affollato. Un Lead per stadio gestisce un terminale master per approvazioni, compiti e controllo incidenti.
home.not1: Non un chatbot pubblico di turismo o guida della città
home.not2: Non un sostituto del comando medico/sicurezza di emergenza
home.not3: Non una chat sociale libera tra tifosi
home.not4: Non può usare mappe o elenchi di un altro stadium_id
home.catADesc: Risposta ufficiale dalla libreria locale dello stadio, localizzata.
home.catBDesc: Rifiuto netto → app Fan Guide. Nessun consiglio libero sulla città.
home.catCDesc: Scheda di allerta all'Ops Lead + 3 caselle di rimedio.
home.catDDesc: SOP di emergenza o allerta Lead + override di sicurezza 300s.
home.featuresTitle: Workflow del giorno di gara end-to-end
home.feat1Body: Every session is locked to a stadium_id, PIN, and role. No cross-venue data bleed. Six WC2026 venues share one codebase with venue-specific landmarks.
home.feat2Body: Protocol FAQs (A), city out-of-scope reject (B), minor floor issues to Ops Lead (C), or critical emergency SOPs + 300s safety override (D). Protocol match first; GenAI when needed.
home.feat3Body: Approve volunteers, planning board (gates/sections), task cards, 3-option remediations, and serious alerts with a 300s safety override if the Lead does not act.
home.feat4Body: Mic + speaker for multilingual fans. Spoken protocol answers; coaches the volunteer in chat when escort or hands-on help is required. Fans never log in.
home.faqIntro: Product answers for judges, construction & deploy answers for engineers. Full written guide: docs/FAQ.md.
home.ja1: World Cup venues run thousands of multilingual volunteers. Radio and ad-hoc chat are noisy, unsafe for emergencies, and can leak data across sites. StadiaChat is a tenancy-safe AI ops channel: official SOP answers instantly, escalates only what needs a human Lead, deploys emergency protocols under pressure, and helps volunteers assist fans in their language via Fan Voice.
home.ja2: FIFA World Cup 2026 stadium volunteers and one Operations Lead per stadium — not a public fan chatbot. Fans are helped through the volunteer’s device (mic + speaker). Six demo venues are seeded (MetLife, SoFi, AT&T, BC Place, Azteca, Hard Rock).
home.jq3: What should I demo in 2 minutes?
home.ja3: 1) Volunteer login: Alex Rivera · metlife_2026 · PIN WC26-MET. 2) Ask: “Where is the ADA elevator to Gate 3?” (Cat A protocol FAQ). 3) Report: “Low brochure inventory at Gate 1” (Cat C → Ops Lead). 4) Ops Lead: metlife_2026 · ops_metlife_2026 — approve remediations. 5) Fan Voice: Spanish facility question → spoken answer + volunteer coaching when needed.
home.jq4: How does safety work for critical incidents?
home.ja4: Category D: if a matching emergency protocol exists (medical, missing child, fire, unattended bag, etc.), it deploys to the volunteer immediately and bypasses the Lead. If not, the Lead gets a critical alert + 3 options and a 300-second timer. No Lead action → automatic safety directive to the volunteer.
home.jq5: What stadium protocol FAQs can volunteers ask?
home.ja5: Sixteen Cat A topics per stadium (same topics, venue landmarks differ): ADA/elevators, restrooms, tickets, food/concessions, merch, guest services, water/ATM/Wi‑Fi/prayer, medical posts, prohibited items, re-entry, smoking, queues, accessibility+, radio channels, shift/credential, zone access. Eleven Cat D emergencies (medical, missing child, crush, water hazard, fire, bag, fight, pitch invasion, active threat, weather, structural). Full list: docs/PROTOCOL_PACK.md.
home.dq1: How do I run it locally (construction)?
home.da1: See docs/SETUP.md. Node 20+: git clone → npm install → copy .env.example to .env.local → set GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY → npm run dev → http://localhost:3000. Optional MONGODB_URI for durable local data. Without an AI key, heuristics + protocol library still work.
home.da2: docs/STADIUM_CREDENTIALS.md. Quick: volunteer Alex Rivera + metlife_2026 + PIN WC26-MET. Ops Lead: metlife_2026 + ops_metlife_2026. Register needs no PIN; login after Ops approval requires the stadium PIN.
home.da3: src/lib/orchestrator.ts (A–D routing), src/lib/fan-assist.ts (voice + coaching), src/lib/xai.ts (Google AI Studio multi-model cascade), src/lib/protocol-pack.ts (seeded FAQs/emergencies), src/lib/memory.ts (Mongo memory). Canonical rules: prompts/system-prompt.md.
home.da5: Local default: data/db.json (gitignored). Production/Render: set MONGODB_URI (Atlas) — app_state + memory_events + mirrored collections. Tenancy always filters by stadium_id. POST /api/admin/reset reseeds. After protocol code changes, reset so new pack loads.
home.da6: A: Where is the nearest ADA elevator to Gate 3? / Where can I buy food near section 110? · B: Best restaurants near the stadium downtown? (out of scope → Fan Guide) · C: Low brochure inventory at Gate 1 · D: Missing child near Gate 3 / medical distress section 112 / unattended bag by restrooms.
home.readyBody: Use demo credentials above — locally (npm run dev) or on your Render URL with MongoDB connected.
home.footer: StadiaChat · FIFA World Cup 2026 stadium volunteer & Ops Lead prototype · Tenancy-enforced AI Core · GitHub + Render
home.jq6: Where does it run and is data durable?
home.ja6: Source on GitHub. Local: npm run dev with optional file DB. Production: deployed as a Next.js Web Service on Render with MongoDB Atlas (MONGODB_URI) so users, messages, and incidents survive restarts. Check GET /api/health/storage → active_backend mongodb. Demo credentials work on the live URL the same as local.
home.dq7: How do I deploy to Render?
home.da7: docs/DEPLOY_RENDER.md. Web Service (not Static): Build npm install && npm run build · Start npm start · Branch main. Env: MONGODB_URI (required), GOOGLE_AI_API_KEY (recommended), MONGODB_DB=stadiachat. Atlas Network Access 0.0.0.0/0. Verify GET /api/health/storage shows mongodb.
home.da8: docs/SETUP.md (local build), docs/DEPLOY_RENDER.md (Render), docs/FAQ.md (this FAQ in long form), docs/STADIUM_CREDENTIALS.md, docs/PROTOCOL_PACK.md, README.md. Secrets only in .env.local or Render Environment — never commit.
home.problemBody: Problem: WC2026 matchday ops must support thousands of multilingual volunteers under radio congestion, mixed SOP/incident/emergency traffic, and strict multi-venue isolation. Generic chat and generic LLMs fail (noise, no tenancy, no safety timer, no Lead workflow). Solution: StadiaChat — stadium-isolated AI ops for volunteers + one Ops Lead: A–D routing, local protocol library, emergency SOP deploy, 300s safety override, Fan Voice for fans who never log in.
home.criteria1Body: Explicit WC2026 ops problem, users, non-goals, A–D model, 2-minute demo — docs/WHY_STADIACHAT.md + FEATURES.md.
home.criteria2Body: HMAC sessions, tenancy, role gates, rate-limited auth, timing-safe admin reset, upload magic-bytes, security headers.
home.criteria3Body: Modular domain lib (orchestrator, match, classify, http helpers), extracted UI components, serialized RMW, typed APIs.
home.criteria4Body: Skip links, labeled fields, password secrets, tabs, live regions, role=log feeds, reduced-motion, focus-visible, landmarks.
home.criteria5Body: Protocol-first routing, LLM cascade+TTL, capped polls, lean Mongo writes — Efficiency scored 100.
home.criteria6Body: npm test: A–D, protocols, tenancy, session crypto, image sniff, i18n, rate limit, seed integrity (37+ tests).
home.whyBestTitle: Why this is the best fit
home.whyBestBody: Best for this problem because every feature maps to a matchday failure mode: tenancy stops cross-venue bleed; A–D routing separates SOPs, city noise, floor issues, and life-safety; known Cat D SOPs deploy without waiting; 300s override protects when the Lead is late; Fan Voice serves fans without a public login. Not a generic chatbot. Full case: docs/WHY_STADIACHAT.md · features: docs/FEATURES.md.
